# Pull Request

## Description

Brief description of changes in this PR.

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Code refactoring
- [ ] Performance improvement

## Related Issues

Closes #(issue number)
Related to #(issue number)

## Changes Made

Detailed list of changes:

- Change 1
- Change 2
- Change 3

## Testing Performed

### Build Test
- [ ] `make all` completes successfully
- [ ] `make iso` completes successfully
- [ ] No compiler warnings

### Boot Test
- [ ] Boots successfully in QEMU
- [ ] Boots successfully from ISO
- [ ] Tested on VMware (if applicable)

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Existing unit tests pass
- [ ] New unit tests added for new code
- [ ] Test coverage maintained/improved

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Integration tests pass
- [ ] No regression in existing functionality

### System Tests
- [ ] End-to-end tests pass
- [ ] Manual testing completed

## Code Quality

- [ ] Code follows project style guidelines (see CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] Self-review performed
- [ ] Comments added for complex/non-obvious code
- [ ] No debug print statements left in code
- [ ] No commented-out code blocks
- [ ] All functions have documentation comments
- [ ] File complies with 300-line limit (or discussed with maintainer)

## Documentation

- [ ] README.md updated (if needed)
- [ ] Technical specification updated (if needed)
- [ ] Code comments are clear and helpful
- [ ] New functions/modules documented

## Performance Impact

Describe any performance implications:

- Memory usage: [increased/decreased/unchanged]
- Boot time: [affected/unaffected]
- CPU usage: [affected/unaffected]

List any breaking changes and migration steps:

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the style guidelines of this project
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published
- [ ] I have checked my code and corrected any misspellings
- [ ] Commit messages follow the project guidelines
